title: [rootless] `podman ps`  doesnot show running container even though container processes are still running
body:
[information.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/4550018/information.txt)

<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

<!--
not sure if this issue is same as #1680,  
two of my containers have been configured as systemd service, after sometime i realize `podman ps` doesn't report any output, but the processes inside the container are still running as seen by pstree report.

now post reboot, facing further issues as below
podman start <<containername>>
Error: unable to start container "<<containername>>": unable to find user postgres: no matching entries in passwd file
Please suggest how to troubleshoot this issue.

-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
systemd based containers in rootless mode after some hours/days fails to get displayed with `podman ps`
1.

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
`podman ps`  doesnot display the running containers 

**Describe the results you expected:**
it should display the running containers

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
unable to simulate further as podman start on the same container gives below error
Error: unable to start container "containerName": unable to find user postgres: no matching entries in passwd file

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.12
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


```
podman-1.6.4-2.module+el8.1.1+5363+bf8ff1af.x86_64

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
vmware based vm

comments.body:
are you running the container through a systemd service or is systemd running inside of the container?

Is lingering mode enabled for your user?

we are actually trying to run the container through a systemd service, lingering mode is not enabled for the user.
 ls -l /var/lib/systemd/linger
ls: cannot access '/var/lib/systemd/linger': No such file or directory

is it required only during rootless container being run as systemd or for any rootless container?
please share any document link for rootless container, which has the above information, so that i don't miss out on any other prerequisites.

Providing the systemd unit used to launch the container would help greatly.

Also, can you try a `podman ps --all --sync` when this happens and see if the container registers as running

actually i had tried podman ps --all --sync that didn't help, so had to kill the conmon process and rebooted the box, post which starting the container gives the below error
Error: unable to start container "containerName": unable to find user postgres: no matching entries in passwd file

more information is present in the attachment information.txt
any update

if you want to run rootless containers through systemd I suggest to manage their lifecycle through the systemd user session `systemctl --user` or at least make sure lingering mode is enabled.

Setting just `User=` in the systemd service file will not set up the session for the rootless user and will confuse Podman/OCI runtime.

Also the `MemoryMax=3G` and `CPUQuota=20%` limits you've specified do not apply to the container payload as it runs in a different cgroup.  You need to set these limits through Podman.

@giuseppe @mheon 
I am also facing this issue `podman ps` not listing the running container after 3-4 days. 
could you please help me to understand the issue. when I try to get inside the container `podman exec -it elasticsearch /bin/bash` throws 

> Error: cannot exec into container that is not running: container state improper.

### My config details
Podman Version: 1.4.4
AWS RHEL 7.7 & 8.1 
Instance Type: t3a.large 8GB RAM

### Podman run command:
`podman run -d \
-p {{port}}:{{port}} \
--name $CONTAINER_NAME \
-h $CONTAINER_NAME \
-v $CONTAINER_NAME:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data \
-e "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms{{ jvm_heap_size_min }} -Xmx{{ jvm_heap_size_max }}" \
-e "node.master=true" \
-e "node.ingest=true" \
-e "node.data=true" \
$CONTAINER_IMAGE`


`podman@ip-xx-xxx-xxx-xxx[~] $ podman info

@giuseppe ,

i have made note of your point regarding lingering mode,  podman related resource control parameters like memory,cpu in rootless mode give below error, cgroup v2 is still in technical evaluation stages in rhel 8.1 platform, how can we achieve resource control in rootless mode.

demopod@hostname[DEV][~] $ podman create --cpus=2 --name test localhost/postgres:12
Error: invalid configuration, cannot specify resource limits without cgroups v2 and --cgroup-manager=systemd
demopod@hostname[DEV][~] $ podman create --memory=2G --name test localhost/postgres:12
Error: invalid configuration, cannot specify resource limits without cgroups v2 and --cgroup-manager=systemd
demopod@hostname[DEV][~] $ podman create  --name test localhost/postgres:12
90302a4a9eaae56898cd2ea03a9476be20731a8fa21be971760a77e36aa903e2

regards.

unfortunately there is no way of achieving it without cgroup v2, cgroup delegation on cgroup v1 is not safe.

If you need resource control on cgroup v1, then you should use root containers
@giuseppe Will runc be able to support cgroupV2 by RHEL 8.3?

> @giuseppe Will runc be able to support cgroupV2 by RHEL 8.3?

I believe so

> @giuseppe @mheon
> I am also facing this issue `podman ps` not listing the running container after 3-4 days.
> could you please help me to understand the issue. when I try to get inside the container `podman exec -it elasticsearch /bin/bash` throws
> 
> > Error: cannot exec into container that is not running: container state improper.
> 
> ### My config details
> Podman Version: 1.4.4
> AWS RHEL 7.7 & 8.1
> Instance Type: t3a.large 8GB RAM
> 
> ### Podman run command:

@giuseppe could you please provide your comments for this issue. also I have tried the `podman ps --all --sync` but it didn't help
@balamuruganravi Are you managing the container with systemd? Also, any chance you could try a more recent Podman version - 1.4.4 is getting very long in the tooth.

No I am not using systemd for managing the containers.  
I am facing this problem even on `podman version`
```
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.12
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64 
```
Can you confirm that the container is still running? The container process should also have a `conmon` parent; can you check if `conmon` is dead if the container process is not?
Yes the container is running and I am able to access the application URL. can you please provide me the cmd/steps to check the `conmon ` process status

Try and find the PID of the process in the container (looks like a JVM from my initial glance). Then, see what the parent of that PID is - I usually use a tool like htop to do this. We expect every container to have a Conmon process as its parent.

Also, providing `podman inspect` for the container in question may help.

To find the PID of the process executed the `netstat -tulpn | grep LISTEN`  and got the belwo PID
`tcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:9200            0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      20190/slirp4netns`

then executed the ` ps -ef | grep 20190` and got the PPID as 1
> podman   11989 20587  0 05:06 pts/1    00:00:00 grep --color=auto 20190
> podman   20190     1  0 Apr20 ?        00:05:59 /usr/bin/slirp4netns --api-socket /tmp/run-600000/libpod/tmp/dcdbb0ba6c901f47361febcf2e2c8d96f8900437935e7cd51bc2cce626863fcc.net --disable-host-loopback --mtu 65520 --enable-sandbox -c -e 3 -r 4 --netns-type=path /tmp/run-600000/netns/cni-9f29a76d-0791-a48c-8ec2-94244bff42dd tap0

another possibility is that systemd automatically cleans up files under `/tmp` and we lose track of the 
containers.

https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd-tmpfiles.html

Can you show the content of `/tmp/run-600000` when the container disappears?

`ls /tmp/run-600000`

`/tmp/run-600000/libpod/tmp/alive` being suddenly removed (by systemd or otherwise) sounds like a very good candidate here. That would make Libpod assume the system restarted and perform a full state refresh.

Yes. I'm not sure on why it exists suddenly. Could it possibly be a bug depending on the OS version? Is it sure that performing a state refresh will cause login rejections to containers but allow it to run? Trying to understand on how that works..

by default files older than 10 days are periodically deleted from `/tmp` (as configured in `/usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/tmp.conf`).

`/tmp` should be really used only for short lived sessions

I could see the below entry in `/usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/tmp.conf` file. is it causing the problem
```
# Clear tmp directories separately, to make them easier to override
v /tmp 1777 root root 10d
v /var/tmp 1777 root root 30d
```
I would say yes.  Files in that directories can disappear after a certain time.
Yes move your storage to /var/tmp (if that is not cleaned up, or better yet, allocate a directory owner by the user to store storage

Could you please help
To move storage to /var/tmp what file and param/key I should make changes
`/var/tmp` has exactly the same issue.  You need to use a directory not cleaned up by systemd

When I changed path in `/home/podman/.config/containers/storage.conf` to `runroot = "/var/local"` and run the `podman info`
but it still shows the `RunRoot: /tmp/run-600000`  did I miss anything.
No, I believe we still use either /run or /tmp for "temporary" files.  The permanent storage should be in /var/local.
